post.description:
Creates an IOC parsing task to extract STIX objects from free text data or a web page. Currently, this API endpoint does not support parsing of IOCs from files, such as PDF, CSV, XLS, and other files. #### **Request Parameters** Pass the following parameters in the payload of an API request. | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | data | String | Mandatory | Pass the free text data within 50,000 characters or the URL of a web page to extract STIX objects.
If the number of characters in the passed text data or the web page exceeds the character limit, then the initial 50,000 characters are parsed. | | url | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to extract STIX objects from a web page.
If you pass `false`, then the URL passed in the `data` parameter is parsed as free text. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | task_id | String | Returns the ID of the parsing task.
To retrieve the parsing task status, use the _GET Retrieve Parse IOCs Task Status_ API. |

200.description: Parse from text / Parse from URL

get.description:
Returns the status of a parse IOCs task.

#### **Request Parameters**

No parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| status | String | Returns the status of the parsing task. |

The following table describes various status of a parsing task.

| Numeric Status | Status Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | enqueued | The parsing task is in the queue for processing. |
| 1 | running | The parsing task is being processed. |
| 2 | failed | Failed to process parsing due to invalid input. |
| 3 | done | The parsing task has been successfully completed. Use the _GET Retrieve Parsed IOCs_ API to retrieve the parsed IOCs. |
| 5 | delayed | Parsing is delayed due to too many tasks in the queue. Try again after sometime. |
| 6 | canceled | Parsing has been manually cancelled by the user. |
| 7 | aborted | Parsing aborted due to some unexpected error. Create a new parsing task. |

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: [Required] Pass 'status' to retrieve the status of a task. Allowed values: status

parameters.name: num_status
parameters.description: Pass 'true' to retrieve the numeric status  of the task.

200.description: Get parsed IOCs task status - status slug / Get parsed IOCs task status - number status / Task Details / Task Details

put.description:
Updates the details of a task. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | assignee | String | Required | Pass the ID of a user to assign the task. | | text | String | Required | Pass the description of the task to be performed. | | priority | String | Optional | Pass the priority of the task.
Allowed values:
`high`
`medium`
`low`
Default: `Medium` | | status | String | Optional | Pass the status of the task.
Allowed values:
`not_started`
`in_progress`
`completed`Default: `not_started` | | closure_comment | String | Optional (Required if the status is `completed`) | Pass the comment to close the task. | | deadline | Integer | Optional | Pass the deadline of the task. | | type | String | Optional | Pass the type of the threat data object. For example, indicator, malware, vulnerability, campaign, and more. | | object id | String | Optional | Pass the ID of the threat data object to associate the task. Else, the task is created as a global task. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | String | Returns the ID of the task. | | created | Timestamp | Returns the date of creation of the task in EPOCH format. | | modified | Timestamp | Returns the date the task was modified in EPOCH format. | | created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the user who created the task. | | assignee | JSON Object | Returns the details of the assignee of the task. | | text | String | Returns the text or description of the task. | | priority | String | Returns the priority of the task. | | status | String | Returns the status of the task. Status can be in Progress, not started, or completed. | | deadline | Integer | Returns the deadline of the task in EPOCH format. | | closure_comment | String | Returns any comments that are added while marking the task as complete. | | completed_on | Timestamp | Returns the date of completion of the task in EPOCH format. | | type | String | Returns the type of threat data object that is associated with the task. | | object_id | String | Returns the object id associated with the task. |

200.description: Update Task / Update Task / Update Task

Status code 200: Success

get.description:
Retrieves the result after successfully processing the _POST Create Parse IOCs Task API_.

Returns a list of parsed IOCs as STIX objects. Also, returns whether an IOC exists in the Allowed Indicators list in the platform.

## Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

## Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| as | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed autonomous systems. |
| ips | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed IPv4 addresses. |
| emails | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed email addresses. |
| urls | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed URLs. |
| sha256 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA256 hashes. |
| md5 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed MD5 hashes. |
| domains | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed domains. |
| sha1 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA1 hashes. |
| sha224 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA224 hashes. |
| sha384 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA384 hashes. |
| sha512 | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SHA512 hashes. |
| ssdeeps | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed SSDEEP hashes. |
| cve_ids | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed CVE IDs. |
| registry_key_path | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed Windows registry keys. |
| file_paths | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed file paths. |
| directory | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed directories. |
| mac-addr | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed MAC addresses. |
| x509-certificate | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the parsed X.509 certificates. |
| char_limit_exceeded | Boolean | Returns `true` if the number of characters passed in the payload for parsing exceeds 50,000 characters. |
| description | String | Returns the text data passed in the payload for parsing. |

Each parsed IOC object of the response includes the following parameters:

| **Parameter** | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| value | String | Returns the value of an IOC. |
| is_whitelisted | Boolean | Returns `true` if an IOC exists in the Allowed Indicators list in the platform. |

200.description: Parsed IOCs from Text / Parsed IOCs from URL

get.description:
Returns a list of intel added using quick add intel.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| next | String | Returns a link to the next page of the intel history. |
| previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page of the intel history. |
| page_size | Integer | Returns the number of records per page. |
| total | Integer | Returns the number of records available in the quick add history. |
| results | List | Returns the details of the quick add intel history. |

#### The Result Objects

The objects of the Quick Add Intel History include the following parameters.

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| client_collections | Array | Returns a list of client collections in the platform. For quick add intel submissions, this value is `null`. |
| component | String | Returns the component of the intel submission. For quick add intel submission, the value is `quick-add-intel`. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the created date of the intel. |
| created_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the creator of the intel. |
| error_message | String | Returns an error message if the intel creation fails. Else, returns null. |
| feed_id | String | Returns the ID of the intel source. For Quick Add Intel, the default source is Import. |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the quick add intel submission. |
| metadata | JSON Object | Returns the metadata details of the intel, such as the Confidence Score, tags, and description. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the last modified date of the intel. |
| modified_by | JSON Object | Returns the details of the modifier of the intel. |
| published_on | Timestamp | Returns the published date of the intel to a STIX collection in the platform. |
| report_id | String | Returns the ID of the report objected that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. |
| server_collections | Array | Returns a list of server collections in the platform. For quick add intel submissions, this value is `null`. |
| status | String | Returns the intel creation status. |
| title | String | Returns the title of the intel. |
| tlp | String | Returns the TLP of the intel. |

post.description:
Creates intel by providing minimal details of indicators, SDOs, relations, custom objects, and indicators parsed from free text. Default Source: Import Default Collection: Free Text #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | create_intel_feed | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to create an intel feed. Else, pass `false` to create a draft intel.
Default value: `false` | | title | String | Mandatory | Pass a title for the intel within 100 characters. The title is added to the report object that is created as part of the quick add intel submission. | | indicators | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the IOC types and IOC values in key-value pairs. For more information, see _The Indicators Object_. | | metadata | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the additional information to add to the intel. | | metadata.confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass a Source Confidence Score for the intel between 0 and 100. | | metadata.tlp | String | Optional | Pass TLP value for the intel.
Allowed values:
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED`
`WHITE`
`NONE` | | metadata.custom_scores | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the custom score key and the score in key value pairs.
To retrieve the keys of custom scores, use the _GET Custom Scores_ API in the Administration > Configuration > Custom Scores section. | | metadata.description | String | Optional | Pass a description for the intel within 1000 characters. | | metadata.labels | Array | Optional | Pass a list of tag IDs to add to the intel.
To retrieve the list of tags, use the _GET List Tags_ API in the _Tags_ section. | | metadata.is_apply_all | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to apply the metadata to all objects of the intel. Else, pass `false` to apply the metadata to the report object that is created as part of the quick add intel submission only.
Default value: `false` | | parse_indicators | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the IOC types and indicators parsed from free text. For more information, see _The Parse Indicators Object_. | | custom_objects | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the ID of the custom objects and the corresponding custom attributes. For more information, see _The Custom Objects Object_. | | sdos | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the SDO types and comma-separated SDO values. For more information, see _The SDOs Object_. | | observables | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the STIX Cyber Observable Object (SCO) types and comma-separated SCO values. For more information, see _The Observables Object_. | | relations | Array of JSON Objects | Optional | Pass the objects to be associated with the report object created as part of this Quick Add submission.
You can associate a maximum of 10 objects in a single quick add submission.

The following object types are not supported for adding as relations:
**Domain Objects**: Observed Data, Opinion, Report, Note, Custom Object, and Location
**Observables**: Network Traffic and X.509 Certificate
**Indicators**: MD5, SHA1, SHA224, SHA384, SHA256, SHA512, and SSDEEP | | relations.{object index}.name | String | Required | Pass the value of the object. | | relations.{object index}.type | String | Required | Pass the object type. | | relations.{object index}.ioc_type | String | Required if the object type is an indicator | Pass the IOC type if the object type is an indicator | | relations.{object index}.id | String | Required | Pass the unique ID of the object. | **Note**: Atleast one of indicators, sdos, observables, or custom_objects is required to create an intel. #### The Indicators Object The `indicators` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the IOC type as the key and a comma-separated list of IOC values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the IOC value per IOC type. For the allowed list of IOC types and the respective key to pass in the `indicators` object, refer to the following table: | **IOC Type** | **IOC Key** | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ipv4-addr` | | IPv6 | `ipv6-addr` | | Domain | `domain` | | URL | `url` | | Email Address | `email` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | #### The Parse Indicators Object The `parse_indicators` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the IOC type as the key and a list of parsed IOC values as the value. For the allowed list of IOC types and the respective key to pass in the `parse_indicators` object, refer to the following table: | IOC Type | IOC Key | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ips` | | IPv6 | `ipv6` | | Domain | `domains` | | URL | `urls` | | Email Address | `emails` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | | CVE IDs | `cve_ids` | | Windows Registry Key | `registry_key_path` | | File Paths | `file_paths` | | Autonomous System | `as` | | X.509 certificates | `x509-certificate` | | MAC Address | `mac-addr` | #### The Custom Objects Object The `custom_objects` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the ID of a custom object as the key and a list of custom attribute objects as the value. The custom attribute object accepts the name of the primary attribute of a custom object as the key and the attribute value as the value. To retrieve the custom object IDs and the respective primary attribute, use the _GET Custom Object Details_ API in **Administration** > **Custom Entitites Management** > **Custom Objects**. #### The SDOs Object The `sdos` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the STIX Domain Object (SDO) type as the key and a comma-separated list of SDO values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the SDO value per SDO type. For the allowed list of SDO types and the respective key to pass in the `sdos` object, refer to the following table. | SDO Type | SDO Key | | --- | --- | | Vulnerability | `vulnerability` | | Malware | `malware` | | Campaign | `campaign` | | Threat Actor | `threat-actor` | | Intrusion Set | `intrusion-set` | | Attack Pattern | `attack-pattern` | | Incident | `incident` | | Course of Action | `course-of-action` | | Identity | `identity` | | Tool | `tool` | | Infrastructure | `infrastructure` | | Location | `location` | | Malware Analysis | `malware-analysis` | #### The Observables Object The `observables` object in Quick Add Intel accepts the STIX Cyber Observable Object (SCO) type as the key and a comma-separated list of SCO values as the value. You can pass a maximum of 5000 characters as the SCO value per SCO type. For the allowed list of SCO types and the respective key to pass in the `observables` object, refer to the following table. | SCO Type | SCO Key | | --- | --- | | Artifact | `artifact` | | Directory | `directory` | | Email Message | `email-message` | | MAC Address | `mac-addr` | | Mutex | `mutex` | | ​Network Traffic | `network-traffic` | | Process | `process` | | Software | `software` | | User Account | `user-account` | | X.509 Certificate | `x509-certificate` | | File | `file` | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns that the details are successfully added to the intel creation queue. | | task_id | String | Returns the unique ID of the background task that is created to track the intel creation status. |

post.description:
This endpoint imports threat data to Intel Exchange. The formats supported for import are as follows:

| Format | Value | **Allowed File Type** | **Default Collection** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MISP | misp | .json | misp |
| Open IOC | openioc | .xml | openioc |
| STIX 1.x | stix1 | .xml | xml |
| STIX 2.1 | stix2 | .json | Stix2 |
| STIX 2.0 | stix20 | .json | Stix2 |
| STIX 1.x URL | stix1url | URL | url |
| CSV (Recorded Future) | csv-recorded-future | .csv | csv |
| CSV (Cyware) | cy-csv | .csv | csv |

Optionally, you can specify a collection to which the file is imported. If no collection is specified, the data is imported to the default collection.  
To retrieve import collections, use the **GET Collections** API.

Note: For STIX 1.x URL, the body must include the URL value. For more information, see example request.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| details | String | Retrieves the import status details. To confirm if the intel was successfully imported, sign in to the Intel Exchange platform and check the Notifications section. |

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: If the format (path variable) value is stix1 or stix2, enter the STIX version. Default value is 2.1. Allowed values are 1.0, 2.0, and 2.1

collection_id.description: (Optional) Enter the ID of the collection to which the file is imported. Example, 603dd2cf-2c3e-4a6b-8200-505d3586df1f. To retrieve the collection_id, use the GET Collections API. Data is imported to both the default collection as well as this specified collection.

file.description: Select the file to be imported.

details.example: You will be notified once file got uploaded.

value.details: You will be notified once file got uploaded.
You will be notified once file got uploaded.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Enter the format for the import. Allowed values are cy-csv, misp, openioc, stix1, stix2, stix20, stix1url, and csv-recorded-future.

post.description:
# Ingest Bundle with Source and Collection Name

Use this API to ingest a STIX bundle into Intel Exchange by specifying the source and collection name. A STIX bundle can include any combination of STIX Domain Objects (SDOs). For more information on supported objects, see the [STIX documentation](https://docs.oasis-open.org/cti/stix/v2.1/csprd01/stix-v2.1-csprd01.html).

**Note**: If you pass an invalid or missing `source_name` or `collection_name`, the bundle is ingested with the source set to **Import** and the collection set to **STIX2**.

## Request Parameters

| Parameter Key | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| bundle | object | Yes | Pass the STIX bundle that contains one or more STIX objects to ingest. |
| bundle.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the bundle being ingested. |
| bundle.objects | array | Yes | Pass an array of STIX objects included in the bundle. |
| bundle.objects.type | string | Yes | Pass the object type. For example, `indicator`, `malware`, `threat-actor`. |
| bundle.objects.spec_version | string | Yes | Pass the STIX specification version used for the object. |
| bundle.objects.id | string | Yes | Pass a unique identifier for the specific STIX object. |
| bundle.objects.created | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was created. |
| bundle.objects.modified | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp when the object was last modified. |
| bundle.objects.name | string | No | Pass a human-readable name of the object. |
| bundle.objects.indicator_types | array | Yes | Pass the classification types for the indicator. For example, `anomalous-activity`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern | string | Yes | Pass the detection pattern that defines the indicator’s characteristics. For example, `[url:value = 'http: //affected-domains.com/test']`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of pattern used. For example, `stix`. |
| bundle.objects.pattern_version | string | No | Pass the version of the pattern specification being used. |
| bundle.objects.valid_from | string | Yes | Pass the timestamp from which the indicator is considered valid. |
| bundle.objects.object_marking_refs | array | Yes | Pass an array of marking definition references that specify the handling or sharing restrictions for the object. |
| bundle.type | string | Yes | Pass the object type of the bundle. |
| collection_name | string | Yes | Pass the name of the collection that should receive the bundle. |
| source_name | string | Yes | Pass the name of the source from which the bundle originates. |
| source_type | string | Yes | Pass the type of the source. For example, `CUSTOM_STIX_SOURCES`. |

## Response Parameters

| Parameter Key | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| success | boolean | Returns whether the bundle ingestion was successful. |
| message | string | Returns additional details about the ingestion process. |
| data | object | Returns any data generated during the ingestion, if applicable. |
| error_code | string | Returns the error code if the ingestion fails. |
| error_message | string | Returns a description of the error if the ingestion fails. |

Create intel with additional details such as the source and collection. You can also include details for each object, such as description, notes, custom attributes, and more. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | source | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source to map the intel.
If the passed source name does not exists in the platform, then a new source is automatically created in the Miscellaneous source category.
Default source: `Miscellaneous` of the `Miscellaneous` source category. | | collection | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source collection to associate with the source.
If the passed collection name does not exists in the platform, then a new collection is automatically created.
Default collection: Free Text | | metadata | JSON Object | Optional | Pass additional information to associate with the intel. | | metadata.confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass the Source Confidence Score to associate with the intel.
Default value: null | | metadata.tlp | String | Optional | Pass the TLP to associate with the intel.
Allowed values:
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED`
`WHITE`
`NONE`
Default value: null | | metadata.labels | Array | Optional | Pass a list of tag names to associate with the intel.
To retrieve the tags, use the _GET List Tags_ API. | | title | String | Mandatory | Pass the title of the intel within 100 characters. | | all_sdos | Dictionary | Optional | Pass the SDO and IOC objects to add to the intel. For more information, see The _All SDOs Object._ | #### The All SDOs Object The `all_sdos` object accepts `all_iocs` and the SDO type as the key and a list of objects for the respective IOC and SDO type. For a list of the supported SDO types, refer to the following table. | SDO Type | SDO Key | | --- | --- | | Vulnerability | `vulnerabilities` | | Malware | `malwares` | | Campaign | `campaigns` | | Threat Actor | `threat_actors` | | Intrusion Set | `intrusion_sets` | | Attack Pattern | `attack_patterns` | | Incident | `incidents` | | Course of Action | `course_of_actions` | | Identity | `identities` | | Tool | `tools` | | Infrastructure | `infrastructures` | | Location | `locations` | | Malware Analysis | `malware_analysis` | | Custom Objects | `custom_objects` | | File | `files` | | Grouping | `groupings` | | Indicator | `indicators` | | Marking Definition | `marking_definitions` | | Note | `notes` | | Observed Data | `observed_datas` | | Opinion | `opinions` | | Relationship | `relationships` | | Observables | `cyber_observables` | | Software | `softwares` | #### The ALL IOCs Object The `all_iocs` object accepts the IOC type as the key and a list of objects for the respective IOC ype. For a list of the supported IOC types, refer to the following table. | IOC Type | IOC Key | | --- | --- | | IPv4 | `ipv4` | | IPv6 | `ipv6` | | Domain | `domain` | | URL | `url` | | Email Address | `email_address` | | MD5 | `md5` | | SHA1 | `sha1` | | SHA224 | `sha224` | | SHA256 | `sha256` | | SHA384 | `sha384` | | SHA512 | `sha512` | | SSDEEP | `ssdeep` | #### The IOC/SDO Object Pass the following parameters in the JSON object of every IOC or SDO you want add. | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | value | String | Mandatory | Pass the value of an IOC or SDO. | | description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the IOC or SDO within 5000 characters. | | is_false_positive | Boolean | Optional | Pass `true` to mark the IOC or SDO as False-Positive. Default value: `false` | | notes | Array | Optional | Pass a list of note strings to add to the IOC or SDO. | | custom_attributes | JSON Object | Optional | Pass the custom attribute names and the attribute values in key-value pairs.
**Note**: The custom attribute name must start with `x_`. For example, `x_identity` | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns the following success message:
"Intel creation is in progress." |

post.description:
Initiates export of threat data objects to a CSV file based on the CQL input. This API returns a file ID to retrieve the export file download link. You can export a maximum of 100,000 threat data objects in one API request. **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | format | String | Mandatory | Pass the export file format.
Currently, CTIX supports only the `csv` format. | | component | String | Mandatory | Pass the component you want to export.
Currently, CTIX supports only `threat_data`. | | query | String | Mandatory | Pass the CQL query to filter threat data objects.
**Tip**: You can copy the CQL from the CTIX user interface. Press the following keys to copy the CQL code to use in the API request payload:
Windows: Windows + Shift + C
MacOS: Command + Shift + C | | columns | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of columns you want to export.
For the allowed list of columns, see _The Column Object_. | **The Column Object** Each column object includes the key and value of a column. You can include the following columns in the export file. | **Key** | **Value** | | --- | --- | | name | Value | | type | Type | | tlp | TLP | | tags | Tags | | sources | Source | | source_collections | Source Collection | | published_collections | Published Collections | | ctix_created | System Created Date | | ctix_modified | System Modified Date | | country | Country | | analyst_score | Analyst Score | | source_confidence | Source Confidence | **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | details | String | Returns a message that export of the threat data objects is initiated. | | file_id | String | Returns the ID of the export file. You can use this ID to retrieve the download link. |

| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data objects. To get the list of supported object types, see _Supported SDO Types_ in Threat Data > Miscellaneous. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

get.description:
Lists the threat data queries that are saved by the user.

#### **Request Parameters**

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

| next | String | Returns a link to the next page. |
| prev | String | Returns a link to the previous page. |

| results | Array | Returns a list of saved searches. |
| id | String | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. |
| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as `basic` or `cql`. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query of the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the details of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`. |
| shared_users | Array | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then it returns the list of users with whom the saved search is shared. |
| editable | Bool | Returns `true` if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns `false`. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is a threat data search or copied from a reports search, else returns `false`. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns `false`. |

post.description:
Creates a saved search in the CTIX platform. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Mandatory | Pass a name for the saved search. | | query | String | Mandatory | Pass the CQL query of the saved search. | | type | String | Optional | Pass the type of the saved search as `basic` or `cql`. | | shared_type | String | Optional | Pass the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`.
Default: `private` | | shared_users | Array | Optional | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then pass the list of IDs of the users with whom the saved search is shared. | | description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the saved search. | | meta_data | String | Optional | Pass the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | String | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. | | name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. | | type | String | Returns the type of saved search as `basic` or `cql`. | | query | String | Returns the CQL query of the saved search. | | created_by | String | Returns the details of the user who created the saved search. | | shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`. | | shared_users | Array | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then it returns the list of users with whom the saved search is shared with. | | editable | Bool | Returns `true` if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns `false`. | | description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. | | is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is a threat data search or copied from a reports search, else returns `false`. | | meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. | | order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. | | pinned | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns `false`. |

200.description: Create Saved Search / Create Saved Search / Create Saved Search / Create Saved Search

get.description:
Retrieves the details of a saved search.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | String | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. |
| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as `basic` or `cql`. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query of the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the details of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search, such as `private`, `global`, `specific` `users`, or `systems`. |
| shared_users | Array | If the privacy parameter is `specific users,` then it returns the list of users with whom the saved search is shared. |
| editable | Bool | Returns `true` if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns `false`. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is a threat data search or copied from a reports search, else returns `false`. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns `true` if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns `false`. |

200.description: Saved Search Details / Saved Search Details / Saved Search Details / Saved Search Details

put.description:
Updates the saved search data by providing a valid ID.

#### **Request Parameters**

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of the saved search as basic or CQL. |
| name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of the saved search. |
| query | String | Mandatory | Pass the dumped value of the query. |
| shared_type | String | Optional | Pass the privacy parameter of the saved search. It returns a value from private, global, specific users, or systems. |
| description | String | Optional | Pass the description of the saved search. |
| meta_data | String | Optional | Pass the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |

#### **Response Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | UUID | Returns the unique ID of the saved search. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the saved search. |
| type | String | Returns the type of saved search as basic or CQL. |
| query | String | Returns the CQL query corresponding to the saved search. |
| created_by | String | Returns the name of the user who created the saved search. |
| shared_type | String | Returns the privacy parameter of the saved search. It returns a value from private, global, specific users, or systems. |
| shared_users | Array | Returns the list of the users that has access to the saved search, if specific users are selected as a privacy parameter. |
| editable | Bool | Returns True if the user has permission to edit the saved search, else returns False. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the saved search. |
| is_threat_data_search | Bool | Returns True if the saved search is either from threat data search or copied from reports type, else returns False. |
| meta_data | String | Returns the metadata of the saved search that helps in the transformation to the CQL query or threat data filters. |
| order | Integer | Returns the order of the saved search for the user in the listed saved search. |
| pinned | Bool | Returns True if the saved search is pinned by the user, else returns False. |

200.description: Update Saved Search / Update Saved Search / Update Saved Search / Update Saved Search

delete.description:
Deletes a saved search.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

Status code 204: The saved search is deleted successfully.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: [String] Pass the unique ID of a saved search.

put.description:
Updates the order of a pinned saved search.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a pinned saved search. |
| order | Integer | Optional | Pass the order in which the saved search appears. |

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| result | String | Returns success if the saved search is pinned successfully. Else, returns failure. |

post.description:
Pins a saved search to the top.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a saved search. |
| order | Integer | Optional | Pass the order in which you want the saved search to appear. |

#### Response Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| result | String | Returns success if the saved search is pinned successfully. Else, returns failure. |

201.description: Pin Post request / Pin Post request with order

delete.description:
Removes a saved search from the pinned search list.

#### Request Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| saved_search | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a pinned saved search. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 204: The saved search is removed from the pinned search list successfully.

post.description:
Returns a list of threat data objects from the CTIX application. You can retrieve a maximum of 500,000 threat data objects using this API. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | query | String | Mandatory | Pass a CQL query to filter and fetch results. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | analyst_score | String | Returns the score assigned to a threat data object by an analyst. | | analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to a threat data object by an analyst. | | confidence_score | Integer | Returns the score calculated by the CTIX confidence score engine. | | confidence_type | String | Returns the type of confidence.
`ctix`: Confidence score is calculated in CTIX.
`third-party`: Confidence score is calculated by a third-party application. | | country | String | Returns the country name where the threat data object was seen. | | created | Epoch | Returns the date and time of the creation of the threat data object. | | ctix_created | Epoch | Returns the date and time of the creation of the threat data object in CTIX. | | ctix_modified | Epoch | Returns the date and time of modification of the threat data object in CTIX. | | first_seen | Epoch | Returns the date and time at which the threat data object was first seen. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the threat data object. | | indicator_type | String | Returns the type of indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator. | | ioc_type | String | Returns the type of indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator. | | is_actioned | Bool | Returns True if an action was performed on the threat data object, else returns False. | | is_deprecated | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is deprecated, else returns `false`. | | is_false_positive | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is marked as false positive, else returns `false`. | | is_reviewed | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is reviewed, else returns `false`. | | is_revoked | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is revoked, else returns `false`. | | is_whitelisted | Bool | Returns `true` if the threat data object is marked as an allowed indicator, else returns `false`. | | last seen | Epoch | Returns the last seen date and time of the threat data object. | | modified | Epoch | Returns the modified date and time of the threat data object. | | name | String | Returns the name of the threat data object. | | primary_attribute | String | Returns the primary attribute of the threat data object if the threat data object is a custom object. | | published_collections | String | Returns the name of the collections in which the threat data object is published. | | severity | String | Returns the severity of the threat data object. | | source_collections | Array | Returns the list of IDs and names of source collections of the threat data object. | | source_confidence | String | Returns the confidence score of the threat data object as reported by its source. | | sources | Array | Returns the list of sources that reported this threat data object. | | sub_type | String | Returns the sub-type of an object if it is an indicator. | | tags | String | Returns tags defined on the threat data object. | | tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the threat data object. | | valid_from | Epoch | Returns the date and time since when this threat data object is valid. | | valid_until | Epoch | Returns the date and time until when this threat data object is valid. | | enrichments | Dictionary | Returns the details of the last enrichment of the object if the object was enriched |

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: [Integer] Pass the page number to filter threat data objects by page number.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: [Integer] Pass the number of records per page to filter the threat data objects by page size.

parameters.name: page_limit
parameters.description: [Integer] Pass the number of threat data objects to be retrieved.

parameters.name: enrichment
parameters.description: [Boolean] Pass true to retrieve the details of the last enrichment for the objects that can be enriched.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: [String]

parameters.name: component
parameters.description: [Required] Pass "investigation" to retrirve threat data objects that are accessible to use in threat investigations.

parameters.name: nominal
parameters.description: [Required] Pass "true" to retrirve limited details of the threat data objects.

200.description: ListingAPI / Listing with Enrichment / threat-data list / search node list

post.summary: Bulk IOC Lookup and Create Intel
post.description:
Perform a lookup for IOCs to retrieve the list of objects available in the CTIX platform. If some IOCs are not available in the platform, you can choose to create intel and ingest the missing IOCs. You can lookup and ingest a maximum of 1000 IOCs in an API request. You can perform a lookup for the following IOC types: - IPv4 - IPv6 - Email Address - MD5 - SHA1 - SHA256 - SHA512 - SSDEEP - URL - Domain #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ioc_values | Array | Mandatory | Pass a list of up to 1000 IOC values to lookup. | | source | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source to map the intel.
If the passed source name does not exists in the platform, then a new source is automatically created with the suffix `(OpenAPI)` in the `Miscellaneous` source category.
Default source: `OpenAPI Lookup (OpenAPI)` of the `Miscellaneous` source category. | | collection | String | Optional | Pass the name of the source collection to associate with the source.
If the passed collection name does not exists in the platform, then a new collection is automatically created with the suffix `(OpenAPI)`.
Default collection: `OpenAPI Lookup (OpenAPI)`. | | metadata | JSON Object | Optional | Pass additional information to associate with the intel. | | metadata.description | String | Optional | Pass a description for the intel. | | metadata.confidence | Integer | Optional | Pass the Source Confidence Score to associate with the intel.
Default value: null | | metadata.tlp | String | Optional | Pass the TLP to associate with the intel.
Allowed values:
`GREEN`
`AMBER`
`RED`
`WHITE`
`NONE`
Default value: null | | metadata.labels | Array | Optional | Pass a list of tag names to associate with the intel.
By default, the `created_using_openapi_lookup` tag is added to all created objects. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | found_iocs | JSON Object | Returns the details of the IOCs that exist in the platform. | | found_iocs.results | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects. Each object includes the details of the IOCs that exists in the platform. For more information, see _The Results Objects_. | | found_iocs.total | Integer | Returns the details of the IOCs that exist in the platform. | | values_not_found | JSON Object | Returns the details of the IOCs that do not exist in the platform. | | values_not_found.invalid_values | Array | Returns a list of invalid IOC values. Invalid IOC values will not be ingested into the platform. | | values_not_found.total_invalid | Integer | Returns the total count of invalid IOC values. | | values_not_found.valid_iocs | Array | Returns a list of all valid IOC values. All valid IOC values will be ingested into the platform if the query parameter `create=true` is passed with the request. | | values_not_found.total_valid | Integer | Returns the total number of valid IOC values. | #### **The Results Objects** Each object in the `results` array of the response includes the following parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | analyst_cvss_score | Integer | Returns the CVSS score assigned to a vulnerability object by an analyst. | | analyst_score | String | Returns the Confidence Score assigned to the IOC by an analyst. | | analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the IOC by an analyst. | | confidence_score | Integer | Returns the Confidence Score calculated by the CTIX confidence score engine. | | confidence_type | String | Returns the type of Confidence Score.
`ctix`: Confidence Score is calculated in CTIX.
`third-party`: Confidence Score is calculated by a third-party application. | | country | String | Returns the name of the country where the IOC was seen. | | created | Timestamp | Returns the source created date and time of the IOC. | | ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the created date and time of the IOC in CTIX. | | ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the modified date and time of the IOC. | | description | String | Returns the description of the IOC received from the source. | | first_seen | Timestamp | Returns the date and time when the IOC was first seen. | | id | String | Returns the unique ID assigned by CTIX to the IOC. | | inidicator_type | String | Returns the type of an indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator.
Returns `file` for hashes and the indicator type key for other indicators. | | ioc_type | String | Returns the IOC type.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator.
Returns hash type for hashes and the indicator type key for other indicators. | | is_actioned | Boolean | Returns `true` if an action was performed on the threat data object in CTIX. Else, returns `false`. | | is_deprecated | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as deprecated in CTIX. Else, returns `false`. | | is_false_positive | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as false positive in CTIX. Else, returns `false`. | | is_reviewed | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is reviewed. Else, returns `false`. | | is_revoked | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is revoked. Else, returns `false`. | | is_under_review | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked for manual review. Else, returns `false`. | | is_watchlist | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is added to the watchlist. Else, returns `false`. | | is_whitelisted | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as an allowed indicator. Else, returns `false`. | | last seen | Timestamp | Returns the last seen date and time of the IOC. | | modified | Timestamp | Returns the source modified date and time of the IOC. | | name | String | Returns the value of the IOC. | | primary_attribute | String | Returns the primary attribute of the threat data object if the IOC is a custom object. | | published_collections | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the collections in which the IOC is published. | | risk_severity | String | Returns the risk severity of the IOC. For example, UNKNOWN, TRIVIAL, MINOR, MODERATE, MAJOR, and CRITICAL. | | severity | String | Returns the severity level of the IOC. | | source_collections | Array | Returns the list of IDs and names of source collections of the IOC. | | source_confidence | String | Returns the confidence score of the IOC as reported by the source. | | sources | Array | Returns the list of sources that reported the IOC. | | sub_type | String | Returns the sub-type of an indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator.
Returns hash type for hashes and `value` for other indicators. | | subscriber_collections | Array | Returns the collections in which the IOC is published and subscribers are polling data from the collections. | | subscribers | Array | Returns the subscribers that are polling the IOC from published collections. | | tags | Array | Returns the tags associated with the IOC. | | tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the IOC by the source. | | type | String | Returns the SDO type of the threat data object. Returns `indicator` for IOCs. | | valid_from | Timestamp | Returns the date and time since when this IOC is valid. | | valid_until | Timestamp | Returns the date and time until when this IOC is valid. | | enrichments | JSON Object | Returns the last enrichment details of the IOC. |

parameters.name: enrichment
parameters.description: [Boolean] Pass true to retrieve the last enriched details of the IOCs. Default: false

parameters.name: create
parameters.description: [Boolean] Pass true to create an intel for the valid IOCs that are not available on the platform. Default: false

200.description: Bulk Lookup / Bulk Lookup with Invalid IOCs / Bulk Lookup with Create

post.description:
Performs a lookup for threat data objects in the CTIX platform and retrieves the details of the objects, such as basic details, enriched data, and relations. #### **Request Parameters** **Note**: Pass one of the following parameters. | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | value | Array | Optional | Pass a list of up to 100 threat data object values to look up. | | object_id | Array | Optional | Pass a list of up to 100 threat data object IDs to look up. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | analyst_score | String | Returns the score assigned to the threat data object by an analyst. | | analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the threat data object by an analyst. | | country | String | Returns the country name where the threat data object was seen. | | created | Timestamp | Returns the source created date and time of the threat data object. | | ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the created date and time of the threat data object in CTIX. | | ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the last modified date and time of the threat data object in CTIX. | | custom_attributes | Array | Returns a list of custom attributes with details, such as `custom_attribute_name`, `custom_attribute_value`, `custom_attribute_value_integer`, and `custom_attribute_value_float`.
Examples: Custom Attribute Name:`criticality`
Custom Attribute Value: `2` | | confidence_score | Integer | Returns the Confidence Score calculated by the CTIX confidence score engine. | | description | String | Returns the source description of the threat data. | | enrichment_data | Array | Returns a list of enrichment objects retrieved from the enrichment tools. | | first_seen | Timestamp | Returns the first seen date and time of the threat data object. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the threat data object. | | ioc_type | String | Returns the IOC type.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator.
Returns hash type for hashes and the indicator type key for other indicators. | | is_deprecated | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as deprecated in CTIX. Else, returns `false`. | | | is_false_positive | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as false positive in CTIX. Else, returns `false`. | | is_reviewed | Boolean | Returns `true` if the threat data object is manually reviewed. Else, returns `false`. | | is_whitelisted | Boolean | Returns `true` if the IOC is marked as an allowed indicator. Else, returns `false`. | | last seen | Timestamp | Returns the last seen date and time of the threat data object. | | modified | Timestamp | Returns the source modified date and time of the threat data object. | | name | String | Returns the value of the threat data object. | | object_type | String | Returns the SDO type the threat data object type of the IOC. | | published_collections | Array | Returns a list of JSON objects for the collections in which the IOC is published. | | relations | JSON Object | Returns a list of related threat data objects. | | sources | Array | Returns the list of sources that reported the threat data object. | | sub_type | String | Returns the sub-type of an indicator.
Returns `null` if the threat data object is not an indicator.
Returns hash type for hashes and `value` for other indicators. | | tags | Array | Returns the tags associated with the threat data object. | | tlp | String | Returns the TLP assigned to the threat data object by the source. | | manual_review | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data is marked for manual review by an analyst. | | valid_from | Timestamp | Returns the date and time since when this threat data object is valid. | | valid_until | Timestamp | Returns the date and time until when this threat data object is valid. |

parameters.name: enrichment_data
parameters.description: [Boolean] Pass true to retrieve the latest five enrichment data objects. Default: false

parameters.name: relation_data
parameters.description: [Boolean] Pass true to retrieve the latest 100 relations details. Default: false

parameters.name: enrichment_tools
parameters.description: [String] Pass the name of up to five enrichment tools separated by a comma. To retrieve a list of enrichment tools, use the GET Enrichment Tools API under Administration > Enrichment Management > Enrichment Tools.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: [String] Pass a comma-separated list of field names to retrieve specific details of the objects. By default, all fields are retrieved.

get.description:
Returns basic correlated details of a threat data object in CTIX.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| analyst_description | String | Returns the description given by an analyst. |
| analyst_score | Integer | Returns the score given by an analyst. |
| analyst_tlp | String | Returns the TLP value given by an analyst. |
| base_type | Sring | Returns if the object was part of any SDO or has been observed. |
| confidence_score | Integer | Returns the confidence score of the object. |
| confidence_type | String | Returns the type of confidence score engine used to calculate the score. |
| country | String | Returns the country for a valid object. |
| created | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined creation date in EPOCH format of the object. |
| ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the creation date in EPOCH format of the object in the CTIX platform . |
| ctix_modified | Timestamp | Returns the modification date in EPOCH format of the object in the CTIX platform. |
| ctix_score | Integer | Returns the score of an object in the CTIX platform. |
| ctix_tlp | String | Returns the TLP of the object in the CTIX platform. |
| defang_analyst_description | String | Returns the defanged description. |
| description | String | Returns the description of the object. |
| fang_analyst_description | String | Returns the fanged description of the object. |
| first_seen | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined date in EPOCH format on which the object was first observed. |
| last_seen | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined date in EPOCH format on which the object was last observed. |
| modified | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined date in EPOCH format on which the object was modified. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the object. |
| sources | Array | Returns the list of sources from where the object has been received. |
| id | String | Returns the ID of the source. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the source. |
| source_type | String | Returns the type of sources such as API Feed, RSS Feed, and more. |
| sub_type | String | Returns the valid subtype of the object such as an indicator is a valid subtype is a URL, domain, and more. |
| tld | String | Returns the Top Level Domain(TLD) value for Domain objects. |
| tlp | String | Returns the TLP of the object. |
| type | String | Returns the type of object. |
| types | Array | Returns the types of objects referred to by STIX as indicator types, malware types, and more. |
| valid_from | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined as valid in EPOCH format from the date of an object. |
| valid_until | Timestamp | Returns the STIX defined as valid in EPOCH format until the date of an object. |

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: Pass the type of the object such as indicator, vulnerability, malware, and more.

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: Pass the ID of the object to retrieve the details.

get.description:
Retrieves additional information of a threat data object, such as, kill chain phases and published collections.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any input parameters.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| kill_chain_phases | Array | Returns a list of kill chain phases with the phase ID, kill chain name, and phase name. |
| published_collections | Array | Returns a list of published collections with the ID, action type, name, and published time. |

200.description: Threat Data Object Advanced Details / Threat Data Object Advanced Details / Threat Data Object Advanced Details / Threat Data Object Advanced Details

parameters.description: Pass the object type, such as indicator, campaign, vulnerability, and more.

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: Pass the unique ID of an object.

get.description:
Retrieves kill chain information of a threat data object.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |

post.summary: Create Task in Threat Data Object
post.description:
Creates a task for a threat data object. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | assignee | String | Required | Pass the ID of a user to assign the task. | | text | String | Required | Pass the description of the task to be performed. | | priority | String | Optional | Pass the priority of the task.
Allowed values:
`high`
`medium`
`low`
Default: `Medium` | | status | String | Optional | Pass the status of the task.
Allowed values:
`not_started`
`in_progress`
`completed`Default: `not_started` | | closure_comment | String | Optional (Required if the status is `completed`) | Pass the comment to close the task. | | deadline | Integer | Optional | Pass the deadline of the task. | | type | String | Optional | Pass the type of the threat data object. For example, indicator, malware, vulnerability, campaign, and more. | | object id | String | Optional | Pass the ID of the threat data object to associate the task. Else, the task is created as a global task. | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | String | Returns the ID of the task. | | created | Timestamp | Returns the date of creation of the task in EPOCH format. | | modified | Timestamp | Returns the date the task was modified. | | created_by | JSON Object | Returns the user who created the task. | | assignee | JSON Object | Returns the assignee of the task. | | text | String | Returns the text or description of the task. | | priority | String | Returns the priority of the task. | | status | String | Returns the status of the task. Status can be in Progress, not started, or completed. | | deadline | Integer | Returns the deadline of the task. | | closure_comment | String | Returns any comments that are added while marking the task as complete. | | completed_on | Timestamp | Returns the date of completion of the task in EPOCH format. | | type | String | Returns the type of threat data object that is associated with the task. | | object_id | String | Returns the object id associated with the task. |

get.description:
Retrieves the status of the quick actions performed on a threat data object.

#### Request Parameters

This API endpoint does not require any request parameter.

#### Response Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ctix_created | Timestamp | Returns the created date and time of the threat data object in EPOCH format. |
| is_deprecated | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is deprecated, else returns false. |
| is_false_positive | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is marked false positive, else returns false. |
| is_reviewed | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is reviewed, else returns false. |
| is_revoked | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is revoked, else returns false. |
| is_under_review | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is under review, else returns false. |
| is_watchlist | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is marked as a watchlist keyword, else returns false. |
| is_whitelisted | Boolean | Returns true if the threat data object is added to the allowed indicator list, else returns false. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the threat data object. |
| notes | Array | Returns the list of notes added to the object. |
| sub_type | String | Returns the sub-type of the threat data object. |
| tags | Array | Returns the list of tags attached to the threat data object. |
| type | String | Returns the type of threat data object. |

post.summary: Perform Action on Threat Data Object
post.description:
Performs an action on a threat data object. You can perform the actions, such as deprecate, undeprecate, add analyst TLP, add analyst score, and more.

#### Request Parameter

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| object_id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a threat data object to perform an action. |
| object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of threat data object. |
| data | JSON Object | Optional | Pass additional data to pass with the action. |

#### Response Parameters

Status code 200: Action Successfully Executed

Status code 400: Bad Request (Check if you have passed valid data)

200.description: deprecate indicator / un_deprecate indicator / reviewed indicator / manual_review indicator / whitelist indicator / un_whitelist indicator / watchlist indicator / un_watchlist indicator / false_positive indicator / un_false_positive indicator / analyst_tlp indicator / analyst score indicator / analyst_description indicator / add_tag / add_relation / delete / deprecate malware / un_deprecate malware / reviewed malware / manual_review malware / watchlist malware / un_watchlist

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: [String] Pass the action to be performed. Allowed values: deprecate, un_deprecate, reviewed, manual_review, whitelist, un_whitelist, false_positive, un_false_positive, analyst_tlp, analyst_score, add_tag, add_relation, delete, revoke_intel.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of email messages received from email feed sources. #### Request Parameters This API endpoint does not require any request parameter. #### Response Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns a link to the next page. | | previous | String | Returns a link to the previous page. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the limit of the page. | | total | Integer | Returns the total number of email messages. | | id | String | Returns the unique ID of an email message. | | email_config | String | Returns the unique ID of an email feed source. | | to | String | Returns the username of the receiver. | | to_email | String | Returns the email address of the receiver. | | _from | String | Returns the username of the sender. | | _from_email | String | Returns the email address of the sender. | | subject | String | Returns the subject of the email message. | | date | Timestamp | Returns the date and time in EPOCH format when the email is received. | | is_read | Boolean | Returns true if the email is read, else returns false. | | is_starred | Boolean | Returns true if the email message is bookmarked, else returns false. | | is_delete | Boolean | Returns true if the email message is trashed, else returns false. | | stix_created | Boolean | Returns true if intel is created from this email message, else returns false. | | tenant_id | String | Returns the unique ID of the CTIX instance. | | raw_body | String | Returns the body of the email message. | | is_regulatory_email | Boolean | Returns true if the email is marked as a regulatory email, else returns false. | | folder_id | Integer | Returns the unique ID of the email folder. | | folder_name | String | Returns the name of the email folder. | | has_attachments | Boolean | Returns true if the email message includes attachments, else returns false. | | is_reviewed | JSON | If an email message is marked as reviewed, then it returns the review details of an email message.
Else, this parameter is not returned. | | created | Timestamp | Returns the date and time in EPOCH format when the email is created in CTIX. |

parameters.name: email_config
parameters.description: Pass the ID of the email feed source to retrieve emails from a specific source. By default, emails from all email sources are retrieved. To retrieve the email source ID, use the GET Email Source List API under Administration > Integration management > Email Feed Sources.

parameters.name: is_read
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve read emails only. By default both read and unread emails are retrieved.

parameters.name: is_starred
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve starred emails only. By default both starred and non-starred emails are retrieved.

parameters.name: is_regulatory_email
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve regulatory emails only. By default both regulatory and non-regulatory emails are retrieved.

parameters.name: is_delete
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve trashed emails only. By default, trashed emails are not returned.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Pass the query text to search in the subject and body of emails.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Pass the number of emails to retrieve per page. Default: 10

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pass the page number to retrieve emails. Default: 1

parameters.name: has_attachments
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve emails with attachments.

parameters.name: is_reviewed
parameters.description: Pass true to retrieve reviewed emails only. By default both reviewed and not reviewed emails are retrieved.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of articles from RSS feed sources. #### Request Parameters This API endpoint does not require any input parameter. #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns the link for the next page. | | previous | String | Returns the link for the previous page. | | total | Integer | Returns the total number of RSS feed sources. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the number of elements present on a page. | | results | Array | Returns the list of all RSS feed articles. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the article. | | title | String | Returns the title of the article. | | summary | String | Returns the summary of the article. | | published | Timestamp | Returns the date and time of publishing of the article. | | url | String | Returns the URL of the article. | | media_thumbnail | String | Returns URL of the media thumbnail. | | source | JSON Object | Return details such as ID and name of the source RSS feed.
Contains Default_metadata Key.
Default_Metadata :
Contains
**tlp:** string, values can be AMBER,GREEN,WHITE,RED,NONE .
**score:** confidence score for the soure. Values between 0 to 100. default:100
**tags**: Contains list of tags selected bu user . Contains id,name and some tag related values
**selected:** Boolean indicating wether the default_metadata to be applied to report . If not use default values for the metadata.
**is_apply_all**: Boolean indicating wether to apply metadata to all objects/IOCS created. If not used default values for metadata.
**Default Values: tlp: WHITE, score:100, tags:\[\], selected:true, is_apply_all:tru**e | | date_created | Timestamp | Returns the date of creation of this article in EPOCH format. | | is_read | Boolean | Returns true if the article is read, else returns false. | | bookmarked | Boolean | Returns true of the feed has been bookmarked, else returns false |

post.description:
Creates notes in bulk for multiple threat data objects.

### Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| type | String | Mandatory | Pass the object type. Allowed Values: `threatdata`. |
| text | String | Mandatory | Pass the note content to be added to the specified threat data objects. |
| object_ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass the list of object UUIDs to which the note should be attached. To get the `object_id`, use the List Threat Data endpoint |

### Response Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| message | String | Returns the reason based on the status. |
| status | String | Returns the overall result. For example: "SUCCESS", "PARTIAL SUCCESS", or "FAILED". |
| object_ids | Array | Returns the list of threat data object IDs where note creation failed. This is only returned for PARTIAL SUCCESS and BAD REQUEST responses. |
| errors | Array | Contains details of threat data objects that failed to process. This is only returned for PARTIAL SUCCESS and BAD REQUEST responses. |

post.description:
Retrieve relationship details of threat data objects within CTIX. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ids | Array | Mandatory | Pass a list of threat object IDs. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns the pagination link to the next page. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the number of results retrieved per page. | | previous | String | Returns the pagination link to the previous page. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the threat data object or the relationship type. | | hi | Bool | Returns True if the threat data object is hidden in the threat investigation canvas, else returns false. | | prop | String | Returns additional information about the threat data object or the relationship type. | | t | String | Returns the value of the threat data object or the relationship type. | | type | String | Returns one of the following:
`node`: Indicates the threat data object.
`link`: Indicates the relationship type. | | id1 | String | Returns the ID of the source threat data object. | | id2 | String | Returns the ID of the target threat data object. | | u | String | Returns an endpoint to the image of the threat data object. |

Enrich threat data objects for threat investigation using third-party enrichment tools configured in CTIX. #### Request Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | identifier | String | Mandatory | Pass `ctix` to indicate that the threat data object already exists in the CTIX database. If you pass anything else it indicates a custom object. | | value | String | Mandatory | Pass the value of the threat data object. | | action_slug | String | Mandatory | Pass the slug value of the action. For example, `get_ip`. | | app_slug | String | Mandatory | Pass the slug of an enrichment tool to use for enrichment.
To retrieve the list of enrichment tools, use the _GET
Enrichment Tools_ API. | | type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type or the sub type of the threat data object such as url, domain, ipv4-addr, and more. | | object_type | String | Mandatory | Pass the type of the threat data object such as indicator, vulnerability, and more. | | object_id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of the threat data object. | | raw_data | String | Mandatory | If the node is already enriched then pass the raw data to prevent from getting re-enriched. | #### Response Parameters | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | next | String | Returns the pagination link to the next page. | | page_size | Integer | Returns the number of results per page. | | previous | String | Returns the pagination link to the previous page. | | id | String | Returns the ID of the threat data object or the relationship type. | | hi | Boolean | Returns True if the threat data object is hidden in the threat investigations canvas, else returns False. | | prop | String | Returns any additional information about the threat data object or the relationship type. | | t | String | Returns the value of the threat data object or the relationship type. | | type | String | Returns
`node`: Indicates a threat data object.
`link`: Indicates a relationship type. | | id1 | String | Returns the ID of the source node. | | id2 | String | Returns the ID of the target node. | | u | String | Returns an endpoint to the image of the node. |

post.description:
Creates a threat defender content record. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | rule | String | Mandatory | Pass the rule content. | | tags | List | Optional | Pass the list of tags of objects to apply to the content. | | tags: id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a tag. | | tags: name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of a tag. | | tags: colour_code | String | Mandatory | Pass the color code of a tag in HEX key format.
Allowed values:
`#5236E2 #0068FA #EB9C00 #FF5330 #27865F #C4C81D #00A2C2 #C341E7 #AD6B76 #95A1B1` | | external_variables | List | Optional | Pass the list of external variables in key-value pairs to apply when the file is checked against the created threat defender content, such as string, boolean, and number. | | type | String | Optional | Pass the type of threat defender content.
Allowed values:
`yara` | #### Response Parameters Status Code 201: Successful

rule.example:
rule tdl1 : sample rule
{
 strings:
    $a = "a"
 condition:
  $a and true
}

value.rule:
rule tdl1 : sample rule
{
 strings:
    $a = "a"
 condition:
  $a and true
}

| created | Epoch | Returns the date and time at which the threat defender content was created. |
| modified | Epoch | Returns the date and time the threat defender content was last modified. |
| name | String | Returns the name of the threat defender content. |
| tags | List | Returns the list of tags applied to the threat defender content. Tags consist of tag ID, color code, name of the tag, and more. |

Updates a threat defender content. #### **Request Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | rule | String | Optional | Pass the rule content. | | tags | List | Optional | Pass the list of tags of objects to apply to the content. | | tags: id | String | Mandatory | Pass the ID of a tag. | | tags: name | String | Mandatory | Pass the name of a tag. | | tags: colour_code | String | Mandatory | Pass the color code of a tag in HEX key format.
Allowed values:
`#5236E2 #0068FA #EB9C00 #FF5330 #27865F #C4C81D #00A2C2 #C341E7 #AD6B76 #95A1B1` | | external_variables | List | Optional | Pass the list of external variables in key-value pairs to apply when the file is checked against the created threat defender content, such as string, boolean, and number. | | type | String | Optional | Pass the type of threat defender content.
Allowed values:
`yara` | #### **Response Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | Integer | Returns the unique ID of the threat defender content. | | created_by | Object | Returns the CTIX user details that created the content, such as ID, name, email, and more. | | created | Epoch | Returns the date and time at which the threat defender content was created. | | modified | Epoch | Returns the date and time the threat defender content was last modified. | | name | String | Returns the name of the threat defender content. | | tags | List | Returns the list of tags applied to the threat defender content. Tags consist of tag ID, color code, name of the tag, and more. | | type | String | Returns the type of threat defender content, such as YARA. | | external_variables | List | Returns the list of external variables configured for the threat defender content. | | rule | String | Returns the content string. |
